title: Mechanical Evidence Artifact (v1)
description: Canonical structured-evidence shape for Build Doctrine Principle 7 ('Visibility lives in artifacts, not narration'). One file per task verification, written by adapters/claude-code/scripts/write-evidence.sh OR equivalent tooling. Replaces the prose evidence-block convention with machine-written human-readable artifacts: each mechanical check (typecheck, lint, test, schema-validate, files-in-commit) records its outcome deterministically. Prose evidence remains the escalation path for genuinely-novel work via the optional prose_supplement field. The plan-edit-validator.sh hook accepts either format. File location convention: <plan-dir>/<plan-slug>-evidence/<task-id>.evidence.json (sibling-directory variant) OR <plan-dir>/<plan-slug>-evidence.json (single-file plan-aggregate variant — emitted only when the helper writes to an aggregate). Schema version 1 (initial).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Currently always 1. Validators accept current + N-1 versions for graceful migration.

task_id.description: Plan task identifier (e.g., '3.2', 'A.1', '7'). Must match the task ID used in the plan file's checkbox line. plan-edit-validator.sh greps for this when authorizing checkbox flips.

verdict.description: Aggregate verdict. PASS only when every entry in mechanical_checks is true (or contains a passing detail-string). FAIL when any check failed. INCOMPLETE when a check could not be run (missing dependency, environment unavailable). Mirrors the prose evidence-block 'Verdict:' line.

commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA (7-40 hex chars) the evidence pertains to. Typically HEAD at the time of capture. Empty string permitted ONLY when verdict is INCOMPLETE and no commit has landed yet.

files_modified.description: Repo-relative paths of files modified in the commit (or, when commit_sha is empty + verdict is INCOMPLETE, the working-tree files staged or about to be staged). Empty array is valid only for purely procedural tasks (e.g., 'review the plan') with verdict INCOMPLETE or FAIL.

oneOf.description: True = check passed; false = check failed.

detail.description: Free-form detail string (output excerpt, error summary, schema name, etc.).

command.description: The exact command that produced this outcome (replayable).

exit_code.description: Exit code of the command, when applicable.

mechanical_checks.description: Map of check-name → outcome. Check names are domain-specific (typecheck, lint, test:<name>, schema-valid:<name>, files-in-commit, etc.). Each entry is either a boolean (terse) or an object with passed + optional detail/command/exit_code (rich). The helper script defaults to the rich form so detail strings are captured.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the evidence was captured. Example: '2026-05-05T13:42:00Z'. Used by plan-edit-validator.sh as a freshness signal alongside the file's mtime.

format.description: Replayable format matching runtime-verification-executor.sh's accepted shapes. Mirrors the prose evidence-block 'Runtime verification: <format> <command>' convention.

command.description: The replayable command body (e.g., 'tests/foo.spec.ts::test name', 'curl -fsS http://localhost:3000/api/foo', 'SELECT 1 FROM bar').

expected.description: Expected outcome (PASS / 200 OK / row count >= 1). Optional but strongly recommended.

observed.description: Actual outcome from the most recent replay. May be a short string ('exit 0') or a longer captured excerpt.

before_after.description: For fix-class tasks: marks this entry as the pre-fix reproduction (before) or the post-fix verification (after). Both required for fix-class tasks per task-verifier's reproduction-based-verification rule.

runtime_evidence.description: Optional runtime-verification entries for tasks with observable runtime behavior (UI, API, webhook, migration, state machine). Empty for purely structural tasks (file edits, hook updates, prompt updates). When present, must satisfy task-verifier's runtime-verification requirements table.

prose_supplement.description: Optional escalation path for genuinely-novel work where mechanical checks cannot fully express the verification rationale. Free-form prose — read by human reviewers + the plan-evidence-reviewer agent. Use sparingly: most tasks should be fully expressible via mechanical_checks + runtime_evidence.

verifier.description: Identifier for the entity that wrote the evidence (e.g., 'write-evidence.sh', 'task-verifier-agent', 'manual'). Defaults to 'write-evidence.sh' when emitted by the helper script.

plan_path.description: Repo-relative path to the plan file this evidence pertains to. Captured for cross-reference at session-end and during plan-evidence-reviewer audits.
